question:
Will VfL Wolfsburg win on 2025-11-22?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for November 22, 2025
If VfL Wolfsburg wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.bundesliga.com/en/bundesliga

evidences.title: Bundesliga 2025/26 Tactical Previews: VfL Wolfsburg - Get German Football News
evidences.content: The new trainer has mostly stuck to a 4-2-3-1 formation, rotating plenty on the top two attacking axes. A 3-5-2 constellation in a friendly against Dynamo Dresden produced by far the best results. Bence Dardai worked as a ten behind the double-striker-set of Jonas Wind and Vaclav Cerny. Patrick Wimmer and Andreas Skov Olsen rotated heavily on the wings whilst Maximilian Arnold and Joakim Maehle ran the midfield. This produced goals from both strikers before the half-hour-mark was reached. Brazilian left-back Rogerio is the only VfL player to have scored in the final four test fixtures. ### Winners from camp, Wolfsburg #### Dzenan Pejcinovic, LS [...] ### Tactical scout, Wolfsburg Wolfsburg open up competitive play against fifth-tier-opponents SV Hemelingen on Saturday. Scouts of the friendlies reveal that Simonis is nowhere close to finding a consistent XI yet and there’s plenty of injury issues reverberating about the squad. Forecasting a 4-2-3-1 remains easy enough. Figuring out the specific players who shall start not so much. We’ll nevertheless give it a try. Lineup—VfL Wolfsburg (Projected) [...] Lineup—VfL Wolfsburg (Projected) A Pokal fixture seems an appropriate time to...

evidences.content: Bet now at MERKUR BETS #### Wolfsburg vs. Leverkusen Wolfsburg’s situation is worsening. The 1–2 loss in Bremen was their third straight defeat, and once again they let a controlled match slip in the final minutes. After taking the lead, they offered little relief and were punished late. Leverkusen, on the other hand, produced a dazzling 6–0 display against Heidenheim, deciding the game within the first half hour. Anything other than an away win would be a surprise. Prediction: Away win (2) + over 3.5 goals Bet now at MERKUR BETS #### Augsburg vs. HSV [...] Forecast: A genuine clash at eye level. Dortmund hold home advantage, Stuttgart arrive in better form. Everything points to a close match — likely ending in a draw. ##### RB Leipzig vs. SV Werder Bremen Leipzig suffered a stinging 1–3 setback at Hoffenheim. After a strong opening, they lost structure and clarity, missing the chance to reduce the gap to Bayern. Bremen arrive after a dramatic 2–1 comeback against Wolfsburg, powered by determination and late-game intensity. History strongly favors Leipzig: 11 wins in 17 encounters, only two Bremen victories. With superior quality and record, RB are heavy favorites. Forecast: Lei...

evidences.title: VfL Wolfsburg
evidences.content: Within a year, VfL Wolfsburg secured the local Gifhorn "Gifhorn (district)") championship. In late November 1946, the club played a friendly match against the prominent Gelsenkirchen team Schalke 04 at the Volkswagen-owned stadium, marking its emergence as the company's officially supported team. ### Postwar play [edit] Historical chart of Wolfsburg league performance [...] Historical chart of Wolfsburg league performance The club made slow but steady progress[according to whom?] in the following seasons. They captured[tone] a number of[quantify] amateur level championships, but were unable to advance out of the promotion playoffs until finally breaking through[tone] to the top tier Oberliga Nord in 1954 with a 2–1 victory over Heider SV. Wolfsburg, however, struggled in the top flight, narrowly missing relegation each season until finally being sent down in 1959. When Germany's first professional football league, the Bundesliga, was formed in 1963, Wolfsburg was playing in the Regionalliga Nord (II), having just moved up from the Verbandsliga Niedersachsen (III), reaching the German Amateur Championship Final that same year (0–1 vs. VfB Stuttgart Amat.). [...] ## External links...
